zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Whitmore於1406年出生在英國英格蘭爾瑟爾斯塔斯頓時，他的父親John de Whitmore48歲，母親Alice de Chester46歲。他和Alice Grosvenor至少有2個女兒。他去世於1460年在他的家鄉享年54歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Whitmore于1406年出生在英国英格兰爾瑟爾斯塔斯頓时，他的父亲John de Whitmore48岁，母亲Alice de Chester46岁。他和Alice Grosvenor至少有2个女儿。他去世于1460年在他的家乡享年54岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Whitmore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Whitmoor (Devon) or Whitmore (Staffordshire and Dorset), or from any of numerous other places in England with names denoting a white moor or mere (from Old English hwīt ‘white’ + mōr ‘moor’ or mere ‘pool’).
History: Francis Whitmore settled in Cambridge, MA, before 1648.

variants.name: Whittemore
meanings: History: Thomas Whittemore came from England to Charlestown, MA, in or c. 1639. Amos Whittemore, born in Cambridge, MA, in 1759 was an inventor and gunsmith, and another Thomas Whittemore was born in Boston in 1800; he was a Universalist clergyman and MA legislator.

variants.name: Whitmer
meanings: Americanized form of German   Wittmeyer   or of   Wittmer  .

variants.name: Whitmarsh
meanings: English: habitational name from Whitemarsh, a place in the parish of Sedgehill, Wiltshire, named from Old English hwīt ‘white’ (i.e. ‘phosphorescent’) + mersc ‘marsh’. Compare Whitmore .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Whitmore was born in 1406, in Thurstaston, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John de Whitmore, was 48 and his mother, Alice de Chester, was 46. He had at least 2 daughters with Alice Grosvenor. He died in 1460, in his hometown, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Whitmore est né en 1406, à Thurstaston, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John de Whitmore, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Alice de Chester, avait 46 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 filles avec Alice Grosvenor. Il est décédé en 1460, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 54 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Whitmore 1406 in Thurstaston, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Whitmore 48 und seine Mutter Alice de Chester 46. Er hatte mit Alice Grosvenor mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb 1460 im Alter von 54 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Whitmore è nato il nel 1406 nel Thurstaston, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John de Whitmore, aveva 48 anni e il suo madre, Alice de Chester, aveva 46 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figlie con Alice Grosvenor. Morì nel 1460, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 54 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard de Whitmoreが1406年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアスウルスタストンで生まれた時，父John de Whitmoreは48歳，母Alice de Chesterは46歳であった。彼はAlice Grosvenorとの間に少なくとも2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1460年に故郷で54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Whitmore(이)가 1406년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 스르스타스턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Whitmore(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de Chester(은)는 46세였다. 그는 Alice Grosvenor(와)과 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1460년 그의 고향에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Whitmore nasceu em 1406, em Thurstaston, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John de Whitmore, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Chester, tinha 46 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhas com Alice Grosvenor. Ele faleceu em 1460, em sua cidade natal, com 54 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Whitmore родился в 1406 г. в местности турстастон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John de Whitmore было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de Chester, 46. У него было по крайней мере 2 дочери от Alice Grosvenor. Он умер в 1460 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 54 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Whitmore nació en 1406, en Thurstaston, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John de Whitmore, tenía 48 años y su mamá, Alice de Chester, tenía 46 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijas con Alice Grosvenor. Murió en 1460, en su pueblo, a la edad de 54 años.
